channel.title: Sqwod Daily
channel.description: The business of fitness, every weekday.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 25 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 25 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The fitness industry is getting a biology upgrade
From the Global Wellness Institute unpacking how cortisol literally rewires your judgment under pressure, to Sport Alliance deploying AI across gym operations, to the Health & Fitness Association planting flags across Asia — the throughline is the same: the gap between operators who understand the underlying systems (biological, technological, geographic) and those who just follow trends is widening fast. Your competitors aren't just buying better equipment. They're building better infrastructure for decisions.
And it's not stopping at the gym floor. Loya's anti-grey haircare line and Thailand's sustainability-forward retreat model are both using the same playbook your top clients already trust: science-meets-experience positioning that commands premium pricing. The wellness industry is quietly colonizing beauty, travel, and leadership coaching — and the studios that notice early get to be the connective tissue their clients didn't know they needed.
The rundown
01
Your Brain on a Bad Day: The Biology Behind Big Calls
The Global Wellness Institute is digging into how pressure physically hijacks your decision-making — which means the way you show up to tough conversations with staff or clients isn't just mindset, it's biology.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Crunch Fitness Has a New Boss: Meet Chequan Lewis
Crunch Fitness has named Chequan Lewis as its new CEO in what the company is calling a major leadership move. For operators and studio founders, it's worth watching — leadership changes at a chain this size tend to signal where the big-box market is headed next.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI
Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively transforming how the fitness sector operates through AI — meaning the tech your competitors are quietly testing today could be the standard your studio is judged against tomorrow.
Source: body LIFE →
04
Swiss Brand Loya Is Coming for Your Grey Hairs
Longevity label Loya has launched an anti-grey haircare line, bringing the wellness-meets-science positioning that sells so well in fitness into the beauty aisle. If your clients are already buying into recovery tech and biohacking, this is the next category they'll be spending on.
Source: BeautyNewsDaily →
05
Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Rewriting the Wellness Retreat Playbook
Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into something coaches and retreat operators can't easily replicate at home. If you're scouting locations for an immersive program, this corner of the Gulf is worth more than a casual Google.
Source: lokmattimes.com →
06
Health & Fitness Association Is Taking Its Show to Asia
The Health & Fitness Association is expanding its presence across Asia through presentations at major industry events. For operators eyeing growth beyond their home markets, consider this your cue to pay attention.
Source: Health & Fitness Association →
Sqwod recs
✓ Watch: Track Chequan Lewis's first moves as Crunch CEO — leadership pivots at big-box chains telegraph where franchise incentives, pricing models, and member acquisition strategies are heading for the whole market.
✓ Steal: Borrow Loya's positioning logic: take one recovery or performance service you already offer and reframe it with a longevity or science-backed angle in your next email to clients.
✓ Read: Look up Sport Alliance's AI rollout via Body Life — understanding what's being automated at scale tells you exactly which parts of your operations are becoming table stakes vs. genuine differentiators.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Somewhere in Switzerland, a longevity brand looked at grey hair and thought: that's not aging, that's an unmonetized biohack. Respect.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 24 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 24 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The infrastructure of legitimacy is being built around you
Look at today's feed as a single frame: lifestyle medicine earns clinical credibility in cancer care, AI rewires gym operations, recovery becomes a headline partnership asset, and a new CEO lands at one of the biggest chains in the game. These aren't isolated updates — they're the same story told six ways. The fitness industry is graduating from "nice to have" to essential infrastructure, and the organizations paying attention are staking their claims right now.
For operators, the window to position early is narrowing fast. The coaches who document clinical relevance, the studios that bake recovery into their core offer, the founders who treat AI as a tool rather than a threat — they're not ahead of a trend, they're already inside a structural shift. The ones who wait for the trade press consensus are going to be optimizing for a market that already moved.
The rundown
01
Lifestyle Medicine Is Now a Cancer Care Power Player
The Global Wellness Institute says lifestyle interventions matter across the entire cancer journey — from prevention all the way through survivorship. For coaches and studio owners, that's a signal: your work isn't just wellness fluff, it's clinically relevant territory worth leaning into.
Source: Global Wellness Institute →
02
Crunch Fitness Has a New CEO — Meet Chequan Lewis
Crunch Fitness has tapped Chequan Lewis as its new chief executive in what the chain is calling a major leadership move. For operators watching the big-box space, a fresh face at the top usually signals a strategic reset — worth keeping an eye on where Crunch points next.
Source: Athletech News →
03
Cycling BC Keeps the Recovery Rolling With 360 Athletics
Cycling BC has renewed its partnership with Recovery Room, the sister brand of 360 Athletics — a reminder that for sports orgs and operators alike, the recovery offering is becoming just as important as the ride itself.
Source: Cycling BC →
04
Krabi & Koh Lanta Are Quietly Becoming Wellness Operator Gold
Lokmattimes spotlights how Thailand's southern islands are blending sustainability and authentic hospitality into a compelling wellness travel package. For retreat operators and studio founders eyeing international programming, this region is worth a serious look before everyone else catches on.
Source: lokmattimes.com →
05
Sport Alliance is reshaping the fitness industry with AI
Body Life reports that Sport Alliance is actively changing how the fitness sector works through artificial intelligence — meaning the tech wave isn't coming for coaches and operators, it's already in the building.
Source: body LIFE →
06
A NZ supplement brand just pulled creatine — now what?
A New Zealand supplement brand has yanked creatine from shelves, leaving a gap in a lot of client stacks. Worth knowing your alternatives before someone asks you mid-session.
Source: NZ Herald →
Sqwod recs
✓ Watch: Track where Chequan Lewis steers Crunch in his first 90 days — new big-box CEOs telegraph strategic gaps that independent operators can exploit before the pivot is public.
✓ Steal: Cycling BC's partnership framing is worth copying: they renewed with a recovery brand by treating recovery as core to the sport, not an add-on. Reframe your recovery offering the same way in your next member communication.
✓ Read: Pull the Global Wellness Institute's lifestyle medicine and cancer care findings — even one cited data point makes your coach bios and intake materials significantly harder to dismiss by healthcare referral partners.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
A New Zealand supplement brand pulled creatine from shelves, which is a remarkable way to start a conversation with every client who just asked you what creatine even does. Consider it a free education moment, not a crisis.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 23 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 23 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Connect the dots
The fitness business rewards the second look
An inconclusive autopsy, a property tax bill that quietly compounded, a wearable nobody wore past week two — the theme isn't bad luck, it's deferred attention. Operators who only check the obvious numbers (revenue, retention) keep getting surprised by the stuff living one layer down: how their square footage is assessed, whether the gear they recommend actually sticks, what slips through the cracks during a packed coaching day.
The flip side is also true. Fitbod's CEO has been watching the same signal since day one — resistance training doesn't fade — and that patience is now being validated by where AI coaching tools are placing their bets. The operators winning right now aren't the ones reacting to trends; they're the ones who looked closer, earlier, and kept looking.
The rundown
01
SC Trainer's Autopsy Raises More Questions Than Answers
The autopsy of a South Carolina fitness trainer has deepened the mystery surrounding their death rather than resolving it. For coaches and studio operators, it's a reminder that when something seems off, the full picture rarely surfaces on the first look.
Source: Yahoo →
02
Waukee Kettlestone Gym Owner Settles Tax Dispute With Dallas County
A Waukee, Iowa gym owner has reached a settlement with Dallas County over a property tax dispute, per the Des Moines Register. If you own your space, it's a reminder that how your property is assessed can quietly eat into your margins — worth a periodic audit of what you're actually being taxed on.
Source: The Des Moines Register →
03
Fitbit Air named most comfortable tracker ever worn
Mashable SEA's verdict is in: the Fitbit Air takes the top spot for wearable comfort — worth knowing if you're recommending trackers to clients who've ditched their last one by week two.
Source: Mashable SEA →
04
This Tiny Wearable AI Pin Takes Notes So You Don't Have To
AndroidGuys went hands-on with the Plaud NotePin S, a wearable AI note-taker built for in-person work — for coaches and studio operators who are too busy actually coaching to scribble things down, this one's worth a look.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
From day one to today, Fitbod's CEO says resistance training has never stopped being the core of the fitness business — and it's the same logic fueling the rise of AI coaching tools like Google's Health Coach. If you're a studio owner wondering where to double down, the data keeps pointing to the weight room.
Source: TechRadar →
Policy watch
Property Tax Assessments: The Silent Margin Killer
A Waukee, Iowa gym owner just settled a property tax dispute with Dallas County — a reminder that local assessors don't always classify fitness spaces correctly, and the appeals process exists for a reason. If you own your building, an incorrect assessment can drain margin year after year before anyone notices.
Sqwod recs
✓ Try: Test the Fitbit Air with one or two chronically non-compliant clients — the ones who ditched their last tracker by week two. Comfort is an underrated compliance variable.
✓ Steal: Borrow Fitbod's single-signal discipline: pick the one service (strength, recovery, small-group) that has never stopped growing in your studio and build your next hire or programming cycle around it.
✓ Try: If you're coaching back-to-back and losing session notes, look up the Plaud NotePin S — a wearable AI that captures in-person conversations so you're not choosing between being present and keeping records.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Fitbit's new Air just won 'most comfortable tracker ever worn' — which is a lovely honor that also implies every previous Fitbit was, at some level, a mild annoyance strapped to your wrist. Progress.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Monday, 22 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Monday, 22 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
⇄  Acquired
Peloton
 —
acquired connected Pilates brand Skōp, adding the discipline to its fitness portfolio
Connect the dots
The fitness industry is eating its own homework
Peloton buying a connected Pilates brand, Garmin dropping a $349 runner's watch, and Fitbod's CEO reminding everyone that strength training never left — these aren't separate headlines. They're the same story: hardware and software companies are racing to own the full-stack fitness experience before studios do it themselves. The discipline doesn't matter. The data does.
For operators, the pattern is clear: every category you're not capturing digitally is a category someone with a balance sheet is circling. Connected Pilates just got acquired. Connected strength is already commoditized. The question isn't whether your modality will get the Peloton treatment — it's whether you'll still be relevant when it does.
The rundown
01
Peloton just bought connected Pilates brand Skōp
Peloton has acquired Skōp, a connected Pilates innovator, adding the discipline to its expanding fitness portfolio. For studio operators, it's a signal that connected Pilates is officially too big for the big players to ignore.
Source: Yahoo! Finance Canada →
02
She tried to cancel her gym membership. The billing company didn't care.
A member showed up to cancel in person, found no one there, and has been charged ever since — a reminder that unclear cancellation processes aren't just a member frustration, they're a liability waiting to go viral.
Source: Yahoo Finance →
03
$349 Garmin Forerunner 170 Music: A Lot of Watch
Runner's World is calling Garmin's new Forerunner 170 Music a serious value proposition at $349 — if your clients are asking what to strap on their wrists, you've got an answer ready.
Source: Runner's World →
04
Plaud NotePin S Is the AI Recorder You Can Actually Wear
Plaud's NotePin S is a wearable AI recorder designed to capture notes hands-free — which means coaches and studio founders can finally stop fumbling for their phones mid-session and just talk.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
Fitbod's CEO tells TechRadar that resistance training has been the core of health and fitness since day one — and for coaches and operators wondering why AI health chatbots are suddenly everywhere, the answer starts in the weight room.
Source: TechRadar →
Stat
$349
retail price of the Garmin Forerunner 170 Music
When Runner's World calls a watch a 'serious value proposition' at this price point, your clients will find the review before you do — knowing the answer before the question arrives is the whole job.
Sqwod recs
✓ Steal: Borrow Fitbod's positioning playbook: anchor your programming narrative in strength as the original health behavior, then layer AI or tech on top — not the other way around
✓ Try: Test the Plaud NotePin S for post-session voice notes — capturing coaching insights hands-free means your IP stops living exclusively in your head
✓ Read: Pull up your cancellation policy today and walk through it as if you're a frustrated member at 7pm on a Tuesday — fix whatever breaks
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
A member tried to cancel her gym membership in person, found no one there, and kept getting charged. Somewhere, a billing software salesperson is using this as a case study. The gym is using it as a cautionary tale. The difference is about $12 a month and one very angry post.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Sunday, 21 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Sunday, 21 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
Money movement
⇄  Acquired
Peloton
 —
acquired connected Pilates brand Skōp to broaden its fitness portfolio beyond the bike
Connect the dots
Hardware gets smaller; the fitness stakes get bigger
This week's headlines share a quiet through-line: the gap between 'tech product' and 'fitness product' is closing fast. Peloton swallows a Pilates hardware brand. Oura shrinks its ring and adds blood pressure tracking. An AI pin starts capturing your coaching sessions hands-free. None of these companies started as fitness businesses — they started as hardware or software plays — but every move they make lands squarely in your studio's backyard.
The operator lesson is this: the tech world has decided fitness is a platform, not a destination. When Peloton acquires connected Pilates equipment and Oura lowers the wearable barrier for clients who hate 'gear,' they're not just selling gadgets — they're selling the relationship you thought you owned. The studios that survive this wave will be the ones who use these tools to deepen client stickiness before a big tech brand uses them to replace it.
The rundown
01
Peloton snaps up connected Pilates brand Skōp
Peloton has acquired Skōp, a connected Pilates innovator, as it looks to broaden its fitness portfolio beyond the bike. For studio operators eyeing the connected-equipment space, this signals Pilates is officially too big for anyone to ignore.
Source: Yahoo! Finance Canada →
02
Rajnish Wellness: Big Revenue, Bigger Losses in Q4 FY26
The wellness brand posted a revenue surge last quarter — but heavy losses mean growth alone isn't paying the bills. For studio operators, it's a timely reminder that top-line momentum means nothing if the bottom line is bleeding out.
Source: MarketsMojo →
03
Oura Ring 5 Is 40% Smaller and Now Tracks Blood Pressure
The latest Oura Ring just got a serious hardware upgrade — smaller form factor, plus blood pressure tracking that could make it a much easier sell to clients who hate wearing 'gear.' For coaches and studio operators, wearable buy-in just got a lower barrier.
Source: The Gadgeteer →
04
This AI Pin Records Everything You Say—Hands-Free
The Plaud NotePin S is a wearable AI recorder that captures conversations without you touching your phone. For coaches drowning in session notes and client check-ins, that's a pretty compelling reason to pin something new to your kit bag.
Source: AndroidGuys →
05
Fitbod's founder: strength training still runs the show
From day one to today, Fitbod's CEO says resistance training has never lost its throne — and it's the same logic fueling the rise of AI coaching tools like Google's Health Coach. For operators, the message is clear: build your offering around the iron, and the tech will follow.
Source: TechRadar →
Stat
40%
reduction in Oura Ring 5 form factor size
A 40% smaller ring with blood pressure tracking matters to operators because client wearable adoption has always stalled on comfort and vanity — shrink the hardware and you shrink the objection.
Sqwod recs
✓ Try: Clip a Plaud NotePin S to your next coaching session and let it capture notes hands-free — then compare how much more present you are with the client versus when you're fumbling with your phone.
✓ Steal: Borrow Fitbod's positioning logic: anchor your class or program marketing explicitly around strength training as the core, then layer any new tech or modality as the enhancement — not the headline.
✓ Read: Pull Rajnish Wellness's Q4 FY26 results and use them as a board-meeting prop — nothing focuses a founder's mind on margin like a real-world case of revenue surging while losses do the same.
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Meanwhile in fitness
Peloton, once the poster child for 'we only do the bike,' has now acquired a Pilates brand — proving that in fitness, every company eventually becomes a platform company or becomes a cautionary tale.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 19 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 19 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Meet Maja Hart - CanvasRebel Magazine
Via CanvasRebel Magazine.
Source: CanvasRebel Magazine →
02
Beyond Bookings: Vibefam's $1M Seed Round Signals AI and Fintech's Takeover of Fitness
Via BriefGlance.
Source: BriefGlance →
03
Athlean-X and the business behind his fitness brand
Via AD HOC NEWS.
Source: AD HOC NEWS →
04
DOCKING SOON: On-site Owner and Crew Accommodation AND New Gym at The Boat Works
Via Marine Business News.
Source: Marine Business News →

Play →

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 17 June 2026
item.description: Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 17 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Today's reps: what moved in the industry, minus the corporate snooze — and why you should care.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Wearables cross €84bn — and rings are the fastest-growing slice
The category grows ~9% a year, but smart rings are up 31% YoY. For coaches, that means recovery data is going mainstream — and your clients are already wearing it.
Source: Wearables →
02
The 8.5% that decides if your studio survives
Average boutique churn sits near 8.5%/month and acquiring a member costs ~6x retaining one. The fix isn't more ads — it's the first 30 days. Here's the onboarding math.
Source: Boutique studios →
03
Recovery is eating the supplement aisle
Consumer recovery spend is up 22% and 41% now track sleep. The story of 2026 isn't harder workouts — it's the business of resting better.
Source: Recovery economy →
Sqwod recs
✓ Gear: Apple Watch Series 9 — our verdict + best price. · Sponsored
Play
Sqwod Readiness Tap
How sharp are you today? 30-second reaction test — the same PVT sleep scientists use to measure alertness.
Play →
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 16 June 2026
item.description: Investors double down on fitness, recovery funding surges, AI coaching scales.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 16 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Investors double down on fitness, recovery funding surges, AI coaching scales.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Investors double down on fitness
Capital is rotating back into the category after a quiet stretch. Funded competitors mean faster-moving markets — and richer exit options for operators.
02
Recovery funding surges
Money is chasing rest as hard as it once chased reps. Recovery is becoming its own profit center, not a wellness afterthought.
03
AI coaching scales
Automated programming and check-ins are getting good enough to handle the bottom of your client ladder. Use it to free your time for the high-touch tier that actually pays.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Friday, 12 June 2026
item.description: Studio M&A heats up, wearables add UV tracking, menopause fitness goes mainstream.
item.content:encoded:
Friday, 12 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Studio M&A heats up, wearables add UV tracking, menopause fitness goes mainstream.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Studio M&A heats up
Consolidation is accelerating as larger groups roll up independents. Know your numbers — you're either an acquirer, a target, or roadkill.
02
Wearables add UV tracking
Sensors keep expanding into everyday health metrics, deepening the data clients bring you. The coach's edge is interpretation, not collection.
03
Menopause fitness goes mainstream
A once-taboo market is getting purpose-built programming and real budgets. Specializing here is one of the clearest white-space plays in fitness right now.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Thursday, 11 June 2026
item.description: A big-box CEO exits, a new hydration brand launches, alcohol's risks resurface.
item.content:encoded:
Thursday, 11 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
A big-box CEO exits, a new hydration brand launches, alcohol's risks resurface.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
A big-box CEO exits
Leadership churn at a fitness giant is a tell that the mass-membership model is under strain. Boutique operators should read it as an opening, not just gossip.
02
A new hydration brand launches
Another entrant in the electrolyte gold rush shows how cheap a beverage is to launch — and how crowded the shelf has become. Differentiation now lives in distribution, not formula.
03
Alcohol's risks resurface
Fresh health warnings keep nudging clients toward sober-curious habits. Coaches who frame training as the better high have the cultural wind at their back.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Wednesday, 10 June 2026
item.description: Supplements add creatine, recovery tech expands, creator brands scale up.
item.content:encoded:
Wednesday, 10 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Supplements add creatine, recovery tech expands, creator brands scale up.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Supplements add creatine
Creatine is escaping the bro-science aisle into mainstream wellness SKUs. An easy, evidence-backed upsell for coaches who'd rather sell trust than hype.
02
Recovery tech expands
Saunas, cold plunges and compression are becoming standalone revenue lines, not perks. Increasingly the recovery room can out-earn the weight room.
03
Creator brands scale up
Fitness creators are turning audiences into product lines at speed. The lesson for operators: your following is inventory you haven't monetized yet.
Share Sqwod Daily
Know a coach, trainer, or studio founder who should read this? Forward this issue — that's how Sqwod grows.
Subscribe free →
▶  Prefer to listen? Play the 5-min audio →
Forwarded this? Get it yourself
Five minutes, every weekday. Free.
Subscribe free
Sponsored content is clearly labeled. Sqwod editorial is independent of sponsors.
Sqwod · Berlin · Unsubscribe

item.title: Sqwod Daily — Tuesday, 09 June 2026
item.description: Pilates keeps winning, athletes reject soda, women's health raises $215M.
item.content:encoded:
Tuesday, 09 June 2026 View online →
SQWOD DAILY
Pilates keeps winning, athletes reject soda, women's health raises $215M.
— Sqwod
The rundown
01
Pilates keeps winning
Low-impact, high-margin and endlessly shareable, Pilates keeps stealing share from traditional gyms. If you don't offer a version of it, your churned members already found one.
02
Athletes reject soda
Elite athletes ditching sugary drinks is handing functional-beverage brands their marketing script. Expect the trickle-down to reshape what sells at your front desk.
03
Women's health raises $215M
Capital is finally flowing into a long-ignored market. The studios and apps that design for women specifically — not 'shrink it and pink it' — will own the upside.
Share Sqwod Daily
